issue_title: Failed authentication: java.lang.NullPointerException: Cannot invoke "org.keycloak.models.UserModel.getFederationLink()" because "this.delegate" is null
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [X] I have read and understood the above terms for submitting issues, and I understand that my issue may be closed without action if I do not follow them.

### Area

identity-brokering, ldap

### Describe the bug

I'm using Keycloak v24.0.1 with both
- another keycloak server as an identity provider (let's call that "foo")
- an LDAP User federation (with Kerberos SSO).

I had tested both separately and it worked. But when BOTH are enabled, the following occurs, see the descriptions in the sections below.

As soon as I disable the LDAP user federation, it will work.

I should add, the LDAP user federation is configured as follows:
- Edit mode: UNSYNCED
- Import Users: On
- Sync Registrations: Off


### Version

24.0.1

### Regression

- [X] The issue is a regression

### Expected behavior

Successful login with the "foo" keycloak IDP

### Actual behavior

After the steps described in "How to Reproduce", the GUI shows "An Error occurred", and the log shows:

```
2024-03-20 15:37:29,305 WARN  [org.keycloak.services] (executor-thread-10) KC-SERVICES0013: Failed authentication: java.lang.NullPointerException: Cannot invoke "org.keycloak.models.UserModel.getFederationLink()" because "this.delegate" is null
	at org.keycloak.models.utils.UserModelDelegate.getFederationLink(UserModelDelegate.java:195)
	at org.keycloak.storage.ldap.LDAPStorageProvider.lambda$decorateUserProfile$23(LDAPStorageProvider.java:1115)
	at org.keycloak.userprofile.AttributeMetadata.isSelected(AttributeMetadata.java:132)
	at org.keycloak.userprofile.DefaultAttributes.configureMetadata(DefaultAttributes.java:332)
	at org.keycloak.userprofile.DefaultAttributes.<init>(DefaultAttributes.java:87)
	at org.keycloak.userprofile.DeclarativeUserProfileProvider.createAttributes(DeclarativeUserProfileProvider.java:112)
	at org.keycloak.userprofile.DeclarativeUserProfileProvider.createUserProfile(DeclarativeUserProfileProvider.java:139)
	at org.keycloak.userprofile.DeclarativeUserProfileProvider.create(DeclarativeUserProfileProvider.java:122)
	at org.keycloak.authentication.authenticators.broker.IdpReviewProfileAuthenticator.actionImpl(IdpReviewProfileAuthenticator.java:196)
	at org.keycloak.authentication.authenticators.broker.AbstractIdpAuthenticator.action(AbstractIdpAuthenticator.java:93)
	at org.keycloak.authentication.DefaultAuthenticationFlow.processAction(DefaultAuthenticationFlow.java:151)
	at org.keycloak.authentication.AuthenticationProcessor.authenticationAction(AuthenticationProcessor.java:987)
```

### How to Reproduce?

- Go to the keycloak login form
- Click the button at the bottom to sign in with the "foo" keycloak IDP
- At the "foo" keycloak IDP, choose a user not yet known to our own idp, and without email address, and login.
- After redirect back at our IDP, the "Update Account Information" form shows up. Enter an Email address and click "submit".
- Now the errors occur as described in "Actual behavior".

### Anything else?

_No response_

issue_title: WebAuthN registration broken after upgrading to 24.0.1
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [X] I have read and understood the above terms for submitting issues, and I understand that my issue may be closed without action if I do not follow them.

### Area

authentication/webauthn

### Describe the bug

We had the following settings configured for our WebAuthN policy in 23.0.6:

Attention conveyance preference: Direct
Authenticator Attachment: Cross Platform
Require discoverable credential: Yes
Acceptable AAGUIDs: c1f9a0bc-1dd2-404a-b27f-8e29047a43fd

After upgrading to 24.0.1 users began reporting issues registering their Yubikeys. When I look at the admin panel in 24.0.1, The "Authenticator Attachment" setting has been changed to "Platform". When selecting "Cross Platform" it reverts back to "Platform". I had to change the setting to Not Specified in order for it work with an external USB key.

When users attempt to register a Yubikey with the correct AAGUID they are presented with the following error:

Failed to register your Passkey.
not acceptable aaguid = 00000000-0000-0000-0000-000000000000

This was working fine yesterday when we were on 23.0.6. In the logs we see the following when a user attempts to register their key:

```
Mar 20 16:14:58 server kc.sh[999205]: 2024-03-20 16:14:58,567 WARN  [org.keycloak.authentication.requiredactions.WebAuthnRegister] (executor-thread-5) webauthn-error-registration
Mar 20 16:14:58 server kc.sh[999205]: 2024-03-20 16:14:58,576 WARN  [org.keycloak.events] (executor-thread-5) type="CUSTOM_REQUIRED_ACTION_ERROR", realmId="XXXXX", clientId="account-console", userId="XXXXX", ipAddress="XXXXX", error="invalid_registration", credential_type="webauthn", auth_method="openid-connect", web_authn_registration_error_detail="not acceptable aaguid = 00000000-0000-0000-0000-000000000000", custom_required_action="webauthn-register", response_type="code", web_authn_registration_error="webauthn-error-registration", redirect_uri="XXXXX", remember_me="false", code_id="5700685b-ea97-474c-9998-ede4820bd15f", response_mode="query", username="XXXXX"
```

If I remove the AAGUID from the admin panel the users are able to register the key, but we cannot allow any AAGUID, so this is a big issue for us.

### Version

24.0.1

### Regression

- [X] The issue is a regression

### Expected behavior

Users should be able to register their Yubikey given the settings that worked in 23.0.6.

### Actual behavior

Users are presented with the following error unless we remove the AAGUID from the admin panel:
Failed to register your Passkey.
not acceptable aaguid = 00000000-0000-0000-0000-000000000000

### How to Reproduce?

Login as a user without a WebAuthN credential attached and go through the self registration process.

### Anything else?

_No response_

issue_title: quarkus-next: compilation error after the deprecated API removal from SmallRye
issue_body:
### Description

The SmallRye team [removed a deprecated API](https://github.com/smallrye/smallrye-config/pull/1120) (in the version 3.6.0), from which we still override the `iterateValues()` method within [PropertyMappingInterceptor class](https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/blob/main/quarkus/runtime/src/main/java/org/keycloak/quarkus/runtime/configuration/PropertyMappingInterceptor.java#L81).

It's the reason why quarkus-next workflow[ is currently failing](https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/actions?query=branch%3Aquarkus-next++).

issue_title: JAVA_OPTS are not set under Windows
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [X] I have read and understood the above terms for submitting issues, and I understand that my issue may be closed without action if I do not follow them.

### Area

import-export

### Describe the bug

While I was trying to import a realm at startup, I noticed that the encoding is not set correctly. Under Windows it will use cp1252 as default instead of UTF-8. 

The Problem is the kc.bat Script. It will set and use the JAVA_OPTS Variable in the same block of code. This results in the variable remaining empty in the second part. This means that the variable is not yet set in the second part and the file encoding is not included.

```batch
if not "x%JAVA_OPTS%" == "x" (
  echo "JAVA_OPTS already set in environment; overriding default settings with values: %JAVA_OPTS%"
) else (
  set "JAVA_OPTS=...-Dfile.encoding=UTF-8..."
  ....
  set "JAVA_OPTS=%JAVA_OPTS% %JAVA_OPTS_KC_HEAP%"
)
```



### Version

24.0.1

### Regression

- [X] The issue is a regression

### Expected behavior

Keycloak starts with default encoding UTF-8

### Actual behavior

Keycloak starts with default encoding cp125

### How to Reproduce?

Start keycloak under windows and echo JAVA_OPTS 

### Anything else?

_No response_

issue_title: NPE on External OIDC to Internal Token Exchange when Transient Users feature is enabled
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [X] I have read and understood the above terms for submitting issues, and I understand that my issue may be closed without action if I do not follow them.

### Area

oidc

### Describe the bug

Keycloak is unable to perform an External to Internal Token Exchange when the Identity Provider is configured not to store the users

### Version

24.0.1

### Regression

- [ ] The issue is a regression

### Expected behavior

Keycloak was expected to return a new internal token after verifying the token with the external identity provider

### Actual behavior

NPE:

```
ERROR [org.keycloak.services.error.KeycloakErrorHandler] (executor-thread-13) Uncaught server error: java.lang.NullPointerException: Cannot invoke "org.keycloak.sessions.AuthenticationSessionModel.getParentSession()" because the return value of "org.keycloak.broker.provider.BrokeredIdentityContext.getAuthenticationSession()" is null
	at org.keycloak.protocol.oidc.DefaultTokenExchangeProvider.importUserFromExternalIdentity(DefaultTokenExchangeProvider.java:608)
	at org.keycloak.protocol.oidc.DefaultTokenExchangeProvider.exchangeExternalToken(DefaultTokenExchangeProvider.java:541)
	at org.keycloak.protocol.oidc.DefaultTokenExchangeProvider.tokenExchange(DefaultTokenExchangeProvider.java:165)
	at org.keycloak.protocol.oidc.DefaultTokenExchangeProvider.exchange(DefaultTokenExchangeProvider.java:131)
	at org.keycloak.protocol.oidc.grants.TokenExchangeGrantType.process(TokenExchangeGrantType.java:62)
	at org.keycloak.protocol.oidc.endpoints.TokenEndpoint.processGrantRequest(TokenEndpoint.java:139)
	at org.keycloak.protocol.oidc.endpoints.TokenEndpoint$quarkusrestinvoker$processGrantRequest_6408e15340992839b66447750c221d9aaa837bd7.invoke(Unknown Source)
	at org.jboss.resteasy.reactive.server.handlers.InvocationHandler.handle(InvocationHandler.java:29)
	at io.quarkus.resteasy.reactive.server.runtime.QuarkusResteasyReactiveRequestContext.invokeHandler(QuarkusResteasyReactiveRequestContext.java:141)
	at org.jboss.resteasy.reactive.common.core.AbstractResteasyReactiveContext.run(AbstractResteasyReactiveContext.java:147)
	at io.quarkus.vertx.core.runtime.VertxCoreRecorder$14.runWith(VertxCoreRecorder.java:582)
	at org.jboss.threads.EnhancedQueueExecutor$Task.run(EnhancedQueueExecutor.java:2513)
	at org.jboss.threads.EnhancedQueueExecutor$ThreadBody.run(EnhancedQueueExecutor.java:1538)
	at org.jboss.threads.DelegatingRunnable.run(DelegatingRunnable.java:29)
	at org.jboss.threads.ThreadLocalResettingRunnable.run(ThreadLocalResettingRunnable.java:29)
	at io.netty.util.concurrent.FastThreadLocalRunnable.run(FastThreadLocalRunnable.java:30)
	at java.base/java.lang.Thread.run(Thread.java:840)
```

### How to Reproduce?

* Start keycloak with `--features=preview,transient-users`
* Add a new OIDC Identity Provider 
** Configure Token Exchange permission
** Enable "Do not store users" on the Identity Provider's Advanced Settings
* Create a new Client with Token Exchange Permission

Execute the token exchange call:
```
curl --location 'http://localhost:8080/realms/master/protocol/openid-connect/token' \
--header 'Content-Type: application/x-www-form-urlencoded' \
--data-urlencode 'client_id=SOME_CLIENT' \
--data-urlencode 'client_secret=some_password' \
--data-urlencode 'subject_token=this is my secret' \
--data-urlencode 'subject_issuer=identityProviderAliasHere' \
--data-urlencode 'grant_type=urn:ietf:params:oauth:grant-type:token-exchange' \
--data-urlencode 'subject_token_type=urn:ietf:params:oauth:token-type:access_token'
```


### Anything else?

This is related to pull request https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/pull/24542

issue_title: Update user makes User ID changes when federationLink and LDAP_ID is not set properly
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [X] I have read and understood the above terms for submitting issues, and I understand that my issue may be closed without action if I do not follow them.

### Area

admin/api

### Describe the bug

On an existing keycloak 23 with LDAP user federation (Active directory in production, reproduced with LDAP), upgrading to 24 can lead to unexpected behavior.
User managed attributes enabled and LDAP_ID as user managed attribute

Modifiying federated user can lead to user-id modification (user recreation ?)

### Version

24.0.1

### Regression

- [X] The issue is a regression

### Expected behavior

Updating user profile whithout id changes or error if something is malformed or missing.
If not defined inside the update request, keep the existing federationlink and user.

### Actual behavior

When updating user profile, if attributes are set but not LDAP_ID the user is recreated.

### How to Reproduce?

* Create an user with a legacy federation link as user attributes
* Update that user through API with "attributes" property and without "LDAP_ID" attribute 
* Check user ID before and after

### Anything else?

Exemple of "buggy" update :
```bash
curl --request PUT "$HOST/admin/realms/$REALM/users/$USERID" \
--header 'Content-Type: application/json' \
```

issue_title: Prevent duplicate form submission in Create realm dialog in admin ui
issue_body:
### Description

The current "Create realm" dialog in the admin-ui does allow clicking the "Create" button multiple times, if the server response is slow. This yields error messages like the following, where the first "Create realm" request succeeds, but the second attempt failes with a conflict error.
![image](https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/assets/314690/d35fb11b-231c-4d74-bd8c-1eea1d40da47)


### Discussion

_No response_

### Motivation

This problem happens quite often in practice, especially during Keycloak demos when one wants to create a realm quickly.



### Details

We should disable the "Create" button while the request is pending.

### Area

### Describe the bug

```
  client_id      = "admin-cli"
  username       = "foo"
  password       = "bar"

  client_timeout = 60
}

resource "keycloak_realm" "foo_realm" {
  realm                    = "foo"
  display_name             = "foo realm"
  enabled                  = true
  login_with_email_allowed = true
}

resource "keycloak_authentication_flow" "foo_browser_flow" {
  realm_id    = keycloak_realm.foo_realm.id
  alias       = "foo-browser"
  description = "Browser based authentication for clients"
  provider_id = "basic-flow"
}

resource "keycloak_authentication_execution" "foo_browser_cookie_execution" {
  realm_id          = keycloak_realm.foo_realm.id
  parent_flow_alias = keycloak_authentication_flow.foo_browser_flow.alias
  authenticator     = "auth-cookie"
  requirement       = "ALTERNATIVE"
}


resource "keycloak_authentication_subflow" "foo_browser_subflow_authentication" {
  depends_on        = [keycloak_authentication_execution.foo_browser_cookie_execution]
  realm_id          = keycloak_realm.foo_realm.id
  alias             = "foo-browser-subflow-authentication"
  description       = "foo: lorem ipsum"
  parent_flow_alias = keycloak_authentication_flow.foo_browser_flow.alias
```

The flows and subflows are created according to what is specified in the Terraform file.

Apply the provided Terraform file on a Keycloak clustered deployment with an external Infinispan (see provided config files to configure Infinispan and Keycloak).

issue_title: Declining terms and conditions in account-console results in error
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [X] I have read and understood the above terms for submitting issues, and I understand that my issue may be closed without action if I do not follow them.

### Area

account/ui

### Describe the bug

When a user tries to log into the account console with "terms and conditions" action required and then declines the required action the account console shows an error page.

### Version

24.0.2

### Regression

- [ ] The issue is a regression

### Expected behavior

The user is redirected to the login form when declining the terms and conditions page (like any other client)

### Actual behavior

When declining the "Terms and Conditions" when using the account console the page looks like this:

![keycloak_terms_error](https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/assets/1321231/661e317d-fb48-482b-8652-1df68ad1c850)


### How to Reproduce?

1. start Keycloak v24.0.1 with docker image
2. add new realm (name: test)
3. enable "terms and conditions" required action
4. add a test user via admin ui
6. add "terms and conditions" required action to test user
5. add password to test user
6. go to http://localhost:8080/realms/test/account/
7. login with test user
8. click "Decline" when prompted to accept/decline terms and conditions
9. observe unexpected error page

![keycloak_terms_error](https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/assets/1321231/661e317d-fb48-482b-8652-1df68ad1c850)

### Anything else?

This issue seems to specific to the account-console. I created another OIDC client (just created a new client via the admin ui, accepted any redirect uri) and the error page does not occur when using it. I get redirected to the login form as expected when declining the terms. Outside of the information above Keycloak does not provide a lot of clues in dev mode outside of this event log:

keycloak  | 2024-04-01 13:12:52,229 WARN  [org.keycloak.events] (executor-thread-18) type="CUSTOM_REQUIRED_ACTION_ERROR", realmId="5956ea1c-15c3-46d4-a7f4-6efc23a7b427", clientId="account-console", userId="4f2c9f75-97b7-4f77-9ad3-5d081a6eb5d4", ipAddress="172.20.0.1", error="rejected_by_user", auth_method="openid-connect", custom_required_action="TERMS_AND_CONDITIONS", response_type="code", redirect_uri="http://localhost:8080/realms/test/account/", remember_me="false", code_id="bb341876-a314-4965-a71c-8e1ed5705dc1", response_mode="query", username="test"

issue_title: ConditionalLoaAuthenticator documentation incorrect re: unauthenticated users.
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [X] I have read and understood the above terms for submitting issues, and I understand that my issue may be closed without action if I do not follow them.

### Area

authentication

### Describe the bug

When using the ConditionalLoaAuthenticator, the documentation in Keycloak is as-follows:

> Flow is executed only if the configured LOA or a higher one has been requested but not yet satisfied.

However, this is not true if the current user has not reached any authentication level. This can be seen by this logging line:

https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/blob/96672a2a6d9cdbe7cf9d4499c63ad6c7ed07f3b0/services/src/main/java/org/keycloak/authentication/authenticators/conditional/ConditionalLoaAuthenticator.java#L61

Here's an example of me seeing this in my Keycloak stack - the application requires LoA `10`, and the authenticator instead responded `true` (despite being set at `40`).

```txt
# journalctl -xefu keycloak | grep ConditionalLoa
Apr 02 05:14:44 [snip] kc.sh[9575]: TRACE [org.keycloak.authentication.authenticators.conditional.ConditionalLoaAuthenticator] (executor-thread-57) Condition 'MFA_ALWAYS' evaluated to true due the user not yet reached any authentication level in this session, configuredLoa: 40, requestedLoa: 10
```

This appears to have been introduced in https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/commit/93bba8e338d7c785a4e2968fa54ce312be35ddb4, note that the logic [here](https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/commit/93bba8e338d7c785a4e2968fa54ce312be35ddb4#diff-335ef41c05942a2949a8949236021b1a8398db8f6eefda5e1bdb75b18ef9bc08L51-L52) is different.

Worse yet, debugging this requires turning on `TRACE`-level logging, which results in a significant amount of logging output.

### Version

23.0.7

### Regression

- [X] The issue is a regression

### Expected behavior

The condition should have returned false - even though the user is not authenticated, the LoA requested is less than the configured LoA.

### Actual behavior

The condition returns `true` always, because the user does not have a session yet.

### How to Reproduce?

This should be reproduceable with any version of Keycloak after 18.0.0.

1. Create an authentication flow, and add a Conditional subflow as the first step.
2. Set the condition to an arbitrary LoA >= 2.
3. Perform the authentication flow requesting LoA 1.
4. Observe that the LoA condition passes as `true`, even though the LoA was lower.

### Anything else?

Conditional LoA could use some additional documentation overall. It's unclear if this is expected or not.
I'm using the documentation in Keycloak directly as a source of truth.

Note that my desire here is to have four LoAs:

- `MFA_ALWAYS` - This application requires MFA (and reauthentication) every time it is used. 
- `MFA_MANDATORY` - This application requires MFA (but not reauthentication) every time.
- `MFA_SELECTIVE` - This application may require MFA depending on external factors (e.g. the client's IP address).
- `1FA` - This application does not require MFA in any fashion.

Tagging some folks that worked on #10205 / #10511 which may have introduced this issue: @mposolda @stianst @mabartos

issue_title: Password denylist Doesn't Work As Expected
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [X] I have read and understood the above terms for submitting issues, and I understand that my issue may be closed without action if I do not follow them.

### Area

authentication

### Describe the bug

I am using a password blacklist from the SecLists project, it can be found here:
- [10-million-password-list-top-1000000.txt](https://github.com/danielmiessler/SecLists/blob/master/Passwords/Common-Credentials/10-million-password-list-top-1000000.txt)

I have configured my password policy in Keycloak like so:
> "passwordBlacklist(10-million-password-list-top-1000000.txt) and length(8) and lowerCase(1) and upperCase(1) and digits(1) and specialChars(1) and passwordHistory(3) and forceExpiredPasswordChange(90)"

When I test this out by changing my password, the blacklist function seems pretty hit-or-miss. Sometimes a password will be refused, Keycloak will let me know that it's in the blacklist. Other times the password will be changed even though it is present in the blacklist. 

Because my rules include a capital letter, I have been testing with the following passwords:
- Password1!
- 1Password!

Should the passwords "1Password!", "1Password!" and "1PASSword!" all be blacklisted? I've read the documentation over a couple of times, I believe this may be a bug.

### Version

23.0.4

### Regression

- [ ] The issue is a regression

### Expected behavior

The following passwords should all be blacklisted:
- 1password!
- 1Password!
- 1PASSword!

The blacklist includes the following text:
```
$ ack Password 10-million-password-list-top-1000000.txt|grep !                                                                                 
Password1!                                                                                
1Password!                                                                                
Password!
```

### Actual behavior

These passwords are accepted without producing an error.

### How to Reproduce?

If you use the blacklist and configuration settings detailed above, you will still be able to set these blacklisted passwords.

### Anything else?

_No response_
commit_message:
Deny list lower cases all passwords when loading from file

Closes #28381

We always lower case the inbound password before comparing against the deny list
yet the deny list may contain passwords that contain upper case letters. With
this change we will now convert passwords from the deny list into lower case
while loading, ensuring that more passwords match the deny list.

Signed-off-by: Christopher Miles <twitch@nervestaple.com>

issue_title: SSO Session Idle: session is still active after session idle time expired
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [X] I have read and understood the above terms for submitting issues, and I understand that my issue may be closed without action if I do not follow them.

### Area

oidc

### Describe the bug

I am trying SSO Session Idle scenario
I have:
Token Lifespan: 5 minutes
SSO Session Timeout: 30 minutes
SSO Session Max: 10 hours

If I create session at for example 20:00 then I will have:
access_token expiration to 20:05
refresh_token expiration to 20:30.

Problem is session after 20:30 (user is inactive). It should be invalided but it is not. The session is still valid and I see session valid in administration console too. But the session will invalided next +2 minutes at 20:32.

After doing some research it seems this is standard Keycloak behavior where it keeps 2 min allowance for synchronization between Keycloak cluster and there is no way to change.
https://www.keycloak.org/docs/latest/server_admin/index.html#_idle_timeouts_note

But my environment is not using cluster.  But still it adds 2 mins buffer.
How i can avoid this 2 mins buffer?


### Version

24.0.2

### Regression

- [ ] The issue is a regression

### Expected behavior

For non-cluster env, Keycloak should not add 2mins buffer to remove session when session idle time is reached. 
I would suggest that this can be made as a configuration option to set the deployment type.


### Actual behavior

Irrespective of how keycloak is deployed (cluster or non-cluster), 2 mins buffer is added to session idle time

### How to Reproduce?

As mentioned in description

### Anything else?

_No response_

oidc

### Describe the bug

With [my PR](https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/pull/12043), Keycloak now supports [scope parameter in the refresh flow ](https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/issues/12009).
However, I have not added a fix that I have found after committed my PR.

Refresh token scopes (if it must be returned) and client session scopes must not be changed.
From [Section 6 in OAuth2 RFC](https://www.ietf.org/rfc/rfc6749.txt)

> If a
   new refresh token is issued, the refresh token scope MUST be
   identical to that of the refresh token included by the client in the
   request.

This bug in accepted PR leads to issues like [https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/issues/27878](https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/issues/27878).

However, the [accepted PR](https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/pull/28093) for fixing previous issue brings the specification!
Although offline_access scope is not in scope parameter, it returns it in access scope!
Together with the fact that this PR does not guarantee same refresh token scopes.

I will commit a new PR for fixing the bug.

### Version

24.0.2

### Regression

- [ ] The issue is a regression

### Expected behavior

1. Refresh token scopes must be equal to old refresh token scopes during refresh token flow.
2. offline_access must not returned in access scope during a refresh token scope with offline_access existing in refresh flow and not existing in scope parameter


### Actual behavior

1. Refresh token scopes is not equal to old refresh token scopes during refresh token flow when scope parameter with less scope is requested.
2. offline_access is returned in access scope during a refresh token scope with offline_access existing in refresh flow and not existing in scope parameter

### How to Reproduce?

Do a refresh flow with offline_access exists in refresh token scope and not exists in r=scope parameter.
Client must return refresh token

### Anything else?

_No response_

issue_title: IdP Linking: Usernames sometimes lowercase and sometimes uppercase
issue_body:
### Area

identity-brokering

### Describe the bug

When a user adds an external IdP, the username is sometimes stored as lowercase, sometimes stored as original value.

we map the original idp uid to both the BROKER_ID and the BROKER_USERNAME

BROKER_ID: gets always the correct value from idP (case sensitive)
BROKER_USERNAME: sometimes the original value is stored correctly (case sensitive) and sometimes the username value gets changed to all lowercase.




### Version

24.0.2

### Expected behavior

THe username gets correctly set as case-sensitive value coming exactly as from the IDP.

### Actual behavior

The username sometimes gets stored as original value and sometimes as all lowercase value. We dont see any connections to containing special chars.

### How to Reproduce?

Add an external IDP with an idp username prepocessor and provide a case sensitive (including uppercase) username attribute

### Anything else?

username mapper:
![image](https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/assets/6820505/573a9c75-271e-40ac-aeec-b7837e1f4998)


result in the datbase (all demo IDs and no real world data):
- first red box was translated to all lowercase
- second red example gets stored as original
![image](https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/assets/6820505/97e900e5-de95-497e-9f70-e5f6caaa1129)

issue_title: [LDAPStorageProvider] NPE if user is cached but has been deleted in ldap
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [X] I have read and understood the above terms for submitting issues, and I understand that my issue may be closed without action if I do not follow them.

### Area

ldap

### Describe the bug

If a user tries to log in with an account that was previously cached by Keycloak, but has been deleted or recreated in the meantime, a `java.lang.NullPointerException` is logged and the user is getting an error message `Unexpected error when handling authentication request to identity provider.`

Error: `2024-04-08 15:27:43,364 WARN  [org.keycloak.services] (executor-thread-45) KC-SERVICES0013: Failed authentication: java.lang.NullPointerException: Cannot invoke "org.keycloak.storage.ldap.idm.model.LDAPObject.getDn()" because "user" is null`

<details><summary>Full traceback</summary>

```
2024-04-08 15:27:43,364 WARN  [org.keycloak.services] (executor-thread-45) KC-SERVICES0013: Failed authentication: java.lang.NullPointerException: Cannot invoke "org.keycloak.storage.ldap.idm.model.LDAPObject.getDn()" because "user" is null
	at org.keycloak.storage.ldap.idm.store.ldap.LDAPIdentityStore.validatePassword(LDAPIdentityStore.java:358)
	at org.keycloak.storage.ldap.LDAPStorageProvider.validPassword(LDAPStorageProvider.java:779)
	at org.keycloak.storage.ldap.LDAPStorageProvider.isValid(LDAPStorageProvider.java:865)
	at org.keycloak.credential.UserCredentialManager.lambda$validate$11(UserCredentialManager.java:255)
	at java.base/java.util.Collection.removeIf(Collection.java:576)
	at org.keycloak.credential.UserCredentialManager.validate(UserCredentialManager.java:255)
	at org.keycloak.credential.UserCredentialManager.isValid(UserCredentialManager.java:71)
	at org.keycloak.models.SubjectCredentialManager.isValid(SubjectCredentialManager.java:45)
	at org.keycloak.authentication.authenticators.browser.AbstractUsernameFormAuthenticator.validatePassword(AbstractUsernameFormAuthenticator.java:230)
	at org.keycloak.authentication.authenticators.browser.AbstractUsernameFormAuthenticator.validateUserAndPassword(AbstractUsernameFormAuthenticator.java:151)
	at org.keycloak.authentication.authenticators.browser.UsernamePasswordForm.validateForm(UsernamePasswordForm.java:55)
	at org.keycloak.authentication.authenticators.browser.UsernamePasswordForm.action(UsernamePasswordForm.java:48)
	at org.keycloak.authentication.DefaultAuthenticationFlow.processAction(DefaultAuthenticationFlow.java:151)
	at org.keycloak.authentication.AuthenticationProcessor.authenticationAction(AuthenticationProcessor.java:987)
	at org.keycloak.services.resources.LoginActionsService.processFlow(LoginActionsService.java:364)
	at org.keycloak.services.resources.LoginActionsService.processAuthentication(LoginActionsService.java:335)
	at org.keycloak.services.resources.LoginActionsService.authenticate(LoginActionsService.java:327)
	at org.keycloak.services.resources.LoginActionsService.authenticateForm(LoginActionsService.java:392)
	at org.keycloak.services.resources.LoginActionsService$quarkusrestinvoker$authenticateForm_32b8e198ac3110abd1d5774e83a4cf87858129f4.invoke(Unknown Source)
	at org.jboss.resteasy.reactive.server.handlers.InvocationHandler.handle(InvocationHandler.java:29)
	at io.quarkus.resteasy.reactive.server.runtime.QuarkusResteasyReactiveRequestContext.invokeHandler(QuarkusResteasyReactiveRequestContext.java:141)
	at org.jboss.resteasy.reactive.common.core.AbstractResteasyReactiveContext.run(AbstractResteasyReactiveContext.java:147)
	at io.quarkus.vertx.core.runtime.VertxCoreRecorder$14.runWith(VertxCoreRecorder.java:582)
	at org.jboss.threads.EnhancedQueueExecutor$Task.run(EnhancedQueueExecutor.java:2513)
	at org.jboss.threads.EnhancedQueueExecutor$ThreadBody.run(EnhancedQueueExecutor.java:1538)
	at org.jboss.threads.DelegatingRunnable.run(DelegatingRunnable.java:29)
	at org.jboss.threads.ThreadLocalResettingRunnable.run(ThreadLocalResettingRunnable.java:29)
	at io.netty.util.concurrent.FastThreadLocalRunnable.run(FastThreadLocalRunnable.java:30)
	at java.base/java.lang.Thread.run(Thread.java:840)
```

</details>

### Version

24.0.2

### Regression

- [ ] The issue is a regression

### Expected behavior

- No NullPointerException in the logs
- A clear warning is logged that describes that a cached user was not found in the storage provider, including its identifier.
- The user trying to log in gets the usual `Invalid username or password.` message, instead of this error `Unexpected error when handling authentication request to identity provider.`
- [Optional?] Proper cache invalidation if the user was not found, though I have no idea how complicated that might. This is useful if a user was deleted and recreated. In that case it will be found in the cache by the username but not in the ldap because its `entryUUID` changed

### Actual behavior

A  `java.lang.NullPointerException` is printed in the logs if a user tries to log in with an account that has been cached in Keycloak but deleted in LDAP. The user is shown an error message `Unexpected error when handling authentication request to identity provider.`

### How to Reproduce?

- Add an LDAP user federation provider
- Make sure `Synchronization` is disabled in the provider settings
- Make sure the `Cache` is enabled in the provider settings
- Create an LDAP user
- Log in to Keycloak with that user
- Log out
- Delete the user in LDAP
- Log in to Keycloak with that user -> You should now see the `java.lang.NullPointerException` in the logs
